page_summary: Page 1: Assembly instructions for the Skyline Dresser (New Classic Furniture).

instruction_steps.text: We recommend that you assemble this product with the assistance of another person: this will make the assembly easier, and will help eliminate damage to the product or injury to person during assembly.
Please do not over tighten screws or bolts until the assembly is completed.
Please put all parts on a non-abrasive floor before assembly, and follow the assembly steps to assemble your newly purchased product correctly and efficiently.

inventory.inferred_function: storage and organization furniture (a dresser/chest of drawers)
inventory.function_rationale: The label 'DRESSER' indicates a furniture item intended for storing clothes or personal items. The steps describe general assembly guidance (assistance, avoiding over-tightening, placing parts on a non-abrasive floor) rather than usage details, which aligns with assembling a storage unit rather than a different device.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A wide, low cabinet with multiple front-facing drawer fronts and horizontal handles, designed for storing items, with a beveled top edge and feet at the front corners.
inventory.shape: rectangular box:**front face wider than tall; runs lengthwise along the front; sits on legs/feet
inventory.size_relative: large and low-profile relative to depth; wider than tall; prominent front surface
inventory.thickness_or_depth: moderate cabinet depth with slightly recessed top edge
inventory.edge_profile: sharp rectangular outer edges; beveled edge around the top surface
inventory.surface_texture: finished wood or composite surface; smooth to touch with visible edges and corners
protrusions: drawer handles protruding from each drawer front
grooves_slots_holes: drawer pulls/handles (multiple horizontal) on each drawer front
inventory.symmetry: high bilateral symmetry along vertical centerline; drawers appear evenly sized and arranged
orientation_cues: front-facing drawer fronts; legs oriented downward in typical orientation
top edge may be slightly recessed when touched from above
distinctive_landmarks: multiple horizontal drawer fronts with aligned handles across the front
front face significantly wider than depth; visible legs at bottom front corners

differences: dresser has multiple shallow drawers across the front (not a single large door)
front vertical dimension is greater due to drawer fronts; top edge subtly recessed rather than flat like some cabinets
likely more compact/low-profile platform-style; feet/legs more prominent; top edge may be beveled
older term often implies more ornate or taller piece; drawer fronts may differ in style; generally similar function but feel of construction differs
armoire is typically taller with a single or few doors rather than many shallow drawers; deeper depth and greater overall height
wardrobe is a tall enclosure with a rod and/or shelves; usually fewer or no shallow drawers across the front; much larger footprint

action_relevant_notes: Use tactile cues to confirm: count of drawer fronts by feeling vertical seams; horizontal handles indicate drawers
feel for the recessed top edge to orient top surface
locate feet to confirm front-bottom corners; front-facing handles help identify the drawer fronts
apply light, even pressure to avoid disturbing alignment when placing or tightening components (assembly guidance)
uncertainties: Exact number of drawers not clearly countable due to partial view; drawer front dimensions approximate; depth and exact leg shape may vary slightly in 3D rendering

inventory.inferred_function: Left leg support for the product's frame (one of multiple legs providing stability and load-bearing support)
inventory.function_rationale: The label indicates it is a leg component; typical assembly uses legs to support the product and provide balance. The instruction steps do not specify parts, so inference is based on standard furniture/stand construction where a left leg is paired with a right leg.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A solid rectangular leg block intended to be mounted as the left leg, providing vertical support and stability.
inventory.size_relative: Similar in size to other leg pieces in the parts list icon; compact relative to the full frame
inventory.thickness_or_depth: Moderate thickness consistent with a small support block
inventory.edge_profile: Smooth flat faces with sharp edges; no visible holes or slots on the external surface in the icon
inventory.symmetry: Symmetric block; no distinct asymmetric features visible on the icon
distinctive_landmarks: No holes/slots visible on shown faces

differences: B LEFT LEGS appears as a simpler rectangular block with no visible mounting holes or cutouts on the shown faces; attachment features may be on hidden faces
RIGHT LEGS may show different surface features or holes/slots in other views
B LEFT LEGS is described as a simpler block with no visible mounting features on the shown faces; other LEFT LEGS variants may have different shapes or mounting features visible in icons
B LEFT LEGS lacks visible holes/slots on the external faces; attachment features may be hidden in actual part

action_relevant_notes: Use tactile exploration to confirm flat, featureless faces: feel for a solid rectangular block with consistent thickness.
When identifying among legs, compare to RIGHT LEGS and other LEFT LEGS by noting the absence of external holes/slots in the visible faces.
Handle with care to locate any hidden mounting features on non-visible faces if available in real part.
uncertainties: The image only shows a small icon of the component; actual part may have hidden features (holes, notches) not visible in this view
Exact dimensions cannot be determined from the icon alone

inventory.inferred_function: Structural leg component providing horizontal support and height/stability to the assembled product
inventory.function_rationale: The labeling 'RIGHT LEGS' together with assembly instructions about assembling the product and handling screws/bolts suggests this part is one of the legs that support the product. Its role is to bear weight and keep the product upright, typical of a leg assembly in furniture or similar items.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: Right-side rectangular leg block that supports and elevates the product; a simple, symmetric block with straight edges and no visible mounting features on the shown faces.
inventory.size_relative: small to medium compared to the drawer units in the illustration
inventory.edge_profile: straight edges with right-angled corners; no visible bevels on the visible faces
inventory.surface_texture: smooth (no visible texture cues on the shown faces)
protrusions: none clearly visible on the shown faces; hidden mounting features may exist not visible in this view
inventory.symmetry: appears symmetrical along its vertical plane in the depicted orientation

differences: labeling indicates opposite side; in touch, the part roots/attachment points may be on mirrored faces not clearly visible in this view; this entry notes there are no clearly visible holes or pegs on the shown faces

only available information is the right-side labeling and typical mirror pairing; tactile distinction is unclear from the visible faces; rely on part labeling or mounting features not visible in this view
same basic rectangular leg form but intended for opposite side; orientation markers not visible in this view; confirm during assembly with screw/bolts
generic leg piece; may differ in size or mounting interface; this specific part is identified as RIGHT and matches the described right-side leg block

action_relevant_notes: No mounting holes or pegs visible on the shown faces; mounting features may be hidden or on hidden faces—prepare to align with corresponding hardware during assembly
uncertainties: Exact cross-sectional profile not fully visible due to perspective; no screw holes or mounting features clearly visible on the shown faces; interpretation based on the visible front/side faces only

inventory.inferred_function: Provides vertical support and stability for the assembled product, forming part of the base/frame to bear weight and prevent tipping.
inventory.function_rationale: The label 'SUPPORT LEG' implies it is a leg component that supports the structure from the bottom. A base/support leg typically carries weight and helps keep the unit upright. The observed geometry shows a small, blocky piece with a leveling mechanism, consistent with a foot/leveler used to stabilize the cabinet.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A small rectangular block with a threaded stem and a leveling foot, used as a bottom support for stability.
inventory.shape: rectangular block with an attached leveling foot/footpad and threaded stem
inventory.size_relative: small relative to main cabinet legs
inventory.surface_texture: likely smooth machined surfaces on mounting faces; foot base may be slightly textured for grip
protrusions: threaded stem protrusion extending from block
grooves_slots_holes: threaded hole or bore for stem connection
possible mounting holes on the face
inventory.symmetry: symmetrical along vertical axis in the visible portion

differences: D is smaller and includes a leveling foot/leveler; other parts may be full-height cabinet legs without grinding foot or threaded stem
D is a small adjustable foot/leveler rather than a full-length leg
D is a compact leveler with a threaded stem; furniture feet often lack an adjustable stem in this form
D is not a corner-attached leg; it is a short, upright-ready foot with leveling mechanism
D emphasizes leveling capability at the base rather than a long stabilizing brace
D functions as a single adjustable support point, not a structural brace connection

action_relevant_notes: Grip the rectangular block by its mounting face edge when aligning to the cabinet base.
When attaching, rotate or screw the threaded stem into its receptacle until snug; support height is adjusted via the leveling foot.
Use tactile feedback from the threaded stem and foot base to verify proper engagement and level posture.
uncertainties: Exact cross-section and internal threading details are not fully visible in the crop
whether there are additional hidden mounting features not visible in this view
